page: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, a local 600 GB NVMe SSD, and a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. It is equipped with a single NVIDIA L40S GPU from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 45 GB of dedicated VRAM.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. The instance achieves 13568.43 tokens per second for prompt processing on small models and 15.91 tokens per second for text generation on large 70B models, placing it in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and database operations fall into the average range.

A notable tradeoff is the memory subsystem performance. While small-block cached memory reads are average, large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths are weak, ranking in the bottom 25% at 38.63 GB/sec and 22.82 GB/sec respectively. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a dense allocation of GPU memory and local NVMe storage relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for workloads that require high VRAM without needing massive multi-core CPU scaling.

This instance is highly suited for LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, and machine learning workloads that benefit from the Ada Lovelace architecture. The combination of 128 GB of system memory, a 45 GB GPU, and local NVMe storage also makes it appropriate for real-time rendering, 3D visualization, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance designed for GPU-accelerated workloads. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor offering 16 vCPUs, 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 600 GB local NVMe SSD. The system features a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB of VRAM. Performance benchmarks show top-tier capabilities in LLM inference, processing 13568.43 tokens per second for small models and 885.42 tokens per second for large models. Single-core CPU performance is strong, though multi-core operations and large-block memory bandwidth are average to weak. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient balance of high VRAM and local storage, making it highly suitable for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC CPU, 128 GB RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6e.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 600 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU optimized for LLM inference.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance combining dedicated AMD EPYC processing, local NVMe storage, and NVIDIA Ada Lovelace graphics for high-performance inference.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 45 GB VRAM for accelerated graphics and compute.
AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 16 vCPUs and strong single-core performance.
128 GB of DDR4 system memory and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier LLM inference speeds across small, medium, and large models.
Baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Well-suited for machine learning inference, rendering, and data processing.
